entry.title: How LeverX enhances CAD Data Search for Manufacturing with Joule Studio in SAP Build
entry.content:
LeverX creates intelligent CAD data discovery with Joule Studio in SAP Build

For more than 20 years, LeverX has been helping companies across industries implement, integrate, and customize SAP solutions to meet their unique business needs. Among our many focus areas is manufacturing, where efficient data management plays a crucial role in organizations’ day-to-day productivity.

As an early-stage adopter of Joule Studio, we had the opportunity to explore its capabilities in a project inspired by a semiconductor equipment manufacturer’s real-world problem  — and find out how Joule Studio could help overcome their data management challenges in engineering environments.

Why We Chose Joule Studio

Companies approach us with a wide range of challenges, and in every case, we aim to find innovative, effective solutions that not only solve our clients’  immediate problems but also help them reach their strategic goals.

When SAP introduced Joule Studio in SAP Build for early-stage exploration, we immediately saw an opportunity to address one of the persistent pain points in the manufacturing sector: managing and accessing technical data stored within CAD files.

At our client’s company, CAD engineers spent around 4 hours each week searching for technical data hidden in complex file structures, managing analytics, and organizing design files.

Additionally, around 30% of models (components) are created from scratch rather than utilizing existing components as a foundation, leading to extra time spent on design and potential delays in manufacturing and supply chain processes.

Without a guided design process — one that would pre-define attributes based on historical data and previous experience — overall design time increased by approximately 20%. This manual effort slowed down production, contributed to redundant design work, and affected project timelines, in general.

Although SAP systems handled structured data well, properties stored within CAD files were not easily searchable through standard SAP tools. This gap created an opportunity to test Joule Studio's ability to bridge structured and unstructured data.

Building the Solution

Having previous expertise with SAP Build and SAP BTP solutions, we were excited to be among the first to explore Joule Studio’s capabilities. Joule Studio builds on the familiar low-code and no-code tools of SAP Build, providing us with a streamlined and intuitive environment for developing custom Joule skills and AI Agents.

Custom Joule skills developed in Joule studio are natively designed to work with Joule's conversational AI interface, adapting to user intent effortlessly.

Thus, our goal was to develop a custom Joule  skill that would make it easier for engineers to search and retrieve hidden CAD data — enabling smarter, faster decisions directly within SAP Joule. 

Our solution combined several technologies:

  • DataLark acted as the data extractor and parser, pulling CAD file properties from storage into SAP BTP.
  • SAP AI Coreto manage the machine learning processes and ensure accurate parsing of CAD data
  • Joule Studio was used to create a custom Joule skill integrated into Joule.

To better understand the technical setup behind our solution, have a look at the architecture overview below:

Solution Architecture

The custom Joule skill allowed engineers to search and find CAD file properties that would otherwise remain hidden to standard SAP search tools. In our demo scenario, a Bill of Materials (BOM) Engineer used SAP Joule alongside the "Maintain Bill of Material" application in SAP S/4HANA to:

  1. Plan a BOM with related materials and parameters.
  2. Use the Joule assistant to locate necessary CAD models with the right attributes.
  3. Quickly create a complete and accurate BOM based on the search results.

 

With this setup, we projected potential savings of around 10% of time of each CAD engineer. Over time, this could contribute to faster project deliveries, fewer redundant designs, and more efficient supply chains.

Our Insights and Results

Throughout the project, we found that managing the variety and complexity of CAD file properties was a challenging but rewarding task. Since different models contained diverse property structures, developing a search mechanism that worked reliably across all cases required careful design.

Our solution demonstrated strong potential during early-stage testing. While some fine-tuning will be needed for broader production use, especially as Joule Studio evolves toward General Availability, the initial results 一 savings of up to 4 hours per week per CAD engineer 一 confirmed the value and innovation opportunities Joule Studio offers for unstructured data management in engineering environments.

Looking Ahead: Preparing for AI Agents and New Possibilities With Joule Studio

Even at this early stage, the project showed how Joule Studio could open new possibilities for managing unstructured data across both SAP and non-SAP landscapes. With its extensibility to third-party systems like CAD management tools, Joule Studio is well-equipped to deliver more comprehensive, cross-system intelligence and automation. Looking forward, Joule Studio’s upcoming AI Agent builder will unlock advanced capabilities. For instance, businesses will be able to deploy agents that collaborate intelligently across systems, automate multi-step tasks, and provide proactive recommendations—all while being grounded in a company’s unique data.

At LeverX, we are excited to continue exploring how Joule Studio and AI Agents can bring even more intelligence and automation into daily business processes — not only for CAD engineers but across many other roles that deal with complex, unstructured data.

Want to experience transformation like this at your company? Explore Joule Studio within SAP Build and see how you can drive automation and improve user productivity today!

entry.title: How Pricefx is transforming pricing software with Joule Studio in SAP Build
entry.content:
About Pricefx

At Pricefx, we specialize in pricing management and optimization software, serving industries like discrete manufacturing, process and chemical sectors, and distribution. Our customers range from mid-sized enterprises to global corporations, all seeking to streamline pricing strategies and boost profitability. Our projects typically involve integrating our pricing solutions with enterprise systems like SAP Sales Cloud to empower sales teams with actionable insights. While we hadn’t used SAP Build before, diving into Joule Studio as early-stage adopters was our first hands-on experience with SAP’s low-code tools, and we were impressed by its intuitive setup.

Why We Chose Joule Studio

AI Assistants and Copilots are reshaping how businesses operate, and at Pricefx, we’re no strangers to this trend. We’ve been developing our own Agentic AI Copilot to enhance our pricing software, focusing on delivering value through seamless integration with SAP’s ecosystem.

The use case we targeted was clear: empowering sales representatives with a daily dashboard where they could ask Joule questions like, “Which customers should I prioritize today?” or “What product recommendations fit this customer?” By connecting SAP Sales Cloud with Pricefx’s pricing engine through Joule Studio to Joule, we aimed to enable real-time recommendations, price suggestions, and negotiation guidance — helping drive better margins and more effective sales processes.

How We Used Joule Studio

Joule Studio opened up new possibilities for bringing Pricefx’s pricing knowledge with SAP’s AI landscape. A key challenge we faced was teaching Joule to properly understand pricing-related queries — so that it could recognize what the sales reps were asking and pull the right information from our systems. Joule Skills became the foundation for solving this. With custom Joule skills, we were able to define specific types of requests, map them to Pricefx’s data sources, and guide Joule to respond accurately based on the user’s intent.

Before building the Joule Skill, we first connected Pricefx’s pricing data to SAP Build Actions to make it API-consumable. This setup allowed Joule to easily access the necessary information when executing custom Joule skills, ensuring smooth and reliable data retrieval during conversations.

Out of the many Joule skills we explored during our early work with Joule Studio, one standout example was our product recommendation skill.

Pricefx Product Recommendation

 

This seamless interaction not only saves time but also ensures sales reps have data-driven suggestions at their fingertips. Developing this skill in Joule Studio was surprisingly efficient, thanks to its low-code environment, which let us focus on functionality rather than complex coding.

Looking Ahead with Joule Studio

Our experience with Joule Studio has reinforced our commitment to deepening our SAP partnership. The tool’s low-code approach aligns perfectly with our vision of self-service solutions, making it easier for users to leverage pricing insights without technical barriers. We were particularly impressed by how quickly we could build and deploy Joule skills, which speaks to the power of Joule Studio’s design-time.

In 2025 and beyond, we’re excited to take this further. With SAP’s AI Agents on the horizon in the very near future, we see huge potential to create end-to-end workflows — from insights to recommendations to actions. Our plan is to use Joule Studio to develop AI Agents that help our partners act on pricing data more effectively. This focus on actionable outcomes will continue to drive innovation in how pricing software integrates with daily operations.

We’re thrilled to be part of this journey and can’t wait to see where Joule Studio takes us next.

entry.title: How sovanta uses Joule Studio to take ticketing systems to the next level
entry.content:
Real-World Use Case with SAP Joule Studio

In an era where efficiency and intelligent automation are critical, SAP has introduced Joule – a powerful AI assistant that is redefining the way users interact with SAP systems. But what do real-world use cases look like when it comes to optimizing business processes and supporting employees across departments? As an early-stage adopter of Joule Studio, sovanta put the GenAI tool through its paces. From Extension to Integration to AI – sovanta has been consulting and supporting SAP customers in the cloud environment for 15 years and has developed into a specialized SAP BTP partner. Thanks to our experienced BTP, UX, AI, and Development experts, we have established ourselves as a reliable BTP implementation partner, focusing on innovative, easy-to-use and AI-powered business apps that semelssly integrate with various systems.

Bringing together our SAP and AI experience, we successfully implemented the first use case with Joule Studio. The result? An exciting use case in the Center domain. In this blog post, we’ll explore the background of that use case and how our clients can benefit from Joule going forward.

Joule Studio in SAP Build: What It Can Do

First things first: We took a deep dive into Joule Studio within SAP Build, and we’re excited by what we found. The combination of generative AI and deep integration into SAP systems already makes Joule a powerful tool for our clients. But the real magic happens with the addition of Custom Joule Skills via Joule Studio. These enable truly impactful use cases that can make a tangible difference for businesses.

A particularly interesting aspect – and challenge – is Joule’s chat history. Joule automatically logs conversations to autonomously select the appropriate skill for follow-up questions. However, it heavily depends on the descriptions of each skill to correctly trigger them. As Joule Studio pioneer, we observed that Joule occasionally struggles to trigger the most relevant skill for certain follow-ups. By fine-tuning our skill descriptions, we were able to significantly improve precision and context handling. We are excited to see that SAP is actively working to enhance Skill Matching capabilities, and partner feedback like ours is directly shaping the ongoing evolution of Joule Studio’s intelligence.

The Use Case: Ticketing System Integration

To fully explore Joule Studio’s potential, we developed a realistic use case focused on service centers. This scenario showcases how Joule can retrieve service case information by integrating with an external ticketing system and leveraging Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) for contextual insights. This allows users to instantly access relevant ticket data and receive solution recommendations based on similar past cases – even without relying on Joule’s Document Grounding capabilities, which were not yet available during our early adopter experience.

The Business Challenge

When a customer reports an issue with a product, service teams are confronted with a lot of challenges:

  • Understanding the specifics of the incoming service case, extracting key information from it
  • Quickly finding internal documents that may be relevant to the customer issue
  • Checking if a similar issue was previously reported or resolved

All of these actions require the service team to navigate between various different systems, having to copy and paste information and search terms across these.

The Solution and its Key Benefits

This is where the power of Joule Studio and its ability to connect Joule with various systems comes in handy. Making full use of custom skills, we connected:

  • A ticketing system (e.g., JIRA) via API to retrieve and update service tickets.
  • The sovanta GenAI DocumentChat via API to perform RAG using historical case data.
    Good to know: While native RAG orchestration via Document Grounding is still evolving, Joule Studio already makes it easy to integrate external RAG services through APIs as part of a custom Joule Skill. In our case, we demonstrated how external RAG can be connected— using the sovanta GenAI DocumentChat to bring additional context and insights into the conversation.

We allow Joule to interact with these systems via three separate Joule Skills:

  • Skill 1: Search Tickets: Allows Joule to search for specific Tickets in the ticketing System.
  • Skill 2: Find Past Solutions: Use RAG via a context in the Document Chat that contains past solutions.
  • Skill 3: Update Ticket: Allows Joule to update a specific ticket in the ticketing System.

The flow below shows how these skills work together in a real conversation.

Separating the new Joule skills helps make each step in the process more reliable and easier to control. By keeping actions like searching tickets, finding solutions, and updating cases separate, we make sure Joule picks the right skill depending on the user’s request. This approach also ties closely to the importance of good skill descriptions: when each skill is focused and clearly described, Joule can match follow-up questions much more accurately. It keeps the conversation flow smooth and gives us the flexibility to tweak or improve individual skills later without disrupting the whole setup.

This use case shows how a custom Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) service can already be effectively integrated with Joule Studio, even without native support. As a result, support teams benefit from context-aware, AI-generated responses that boost efficiency and improve the overall customer service experience. By instantly retrieving and analyzing ticket data, Joule enables significantly faster issue resolution. It also supports proactive problem-solving by suggesting solutions based on similar past cases.

Future of AI Agents in Joule Studio

Looking ahead, building AI Agents within Joule Studio will be a key next step. As participants in the SAP Alpha Program for AI Agents, we already see strong potential to extend our use case even further. In the future, AI Agents could completely take over first-level support activities such as autonomously requesting additional information, suggesting possible solutions and reacting to further feedback by customers.

This will also require handling ticket escalations efficiently. In many service centers, one common challenge is identifying when a support ticket needs to be escalated. Service agents often have to manually decide if an issue is urgent enough to involve higher-level support, which can lead to delays or missed escalations. With large ticket volumes, it’s easy for critical issues to get lost among routine cases. AI Agents in Joule Studio could help by proactively monitoring tickets for escalation triggers and forwarding those tickets to the correct second- or third level support experts — taking service center efficiency to the next level. 

Our Final Thoughts on Joule Studio

Joule Studio is proving to be a promising addition to the SAP ecosystem. With the ability to extend capabilities using Custom Skills and integrate external services like sovanta GenAI DocumentChat, it offers a flexible and future-proof way to enhance business processes. Integrating Joule with AI Agents will open up exciting possibilities for realizing advanced use cases within Joule Studio, taking full advantage of multi-skill orchestration and autonomous task planning and handling. At sovanta, we’re excited to continue exploring how our clients can benefit from SAP Joule – today and in the future.

entry.title: Top 9 takeaways #SAPSapphire 2025
entry.content:
1. SAP’s Fly-wheel Concept

It's like that classic physics concept, the flywheel. Getting it started takes a real push, a significant effort, but once it's spinning, inertia and the fundamental laws of motion kick-in. Suddenly, moving forward becomes easier and easier. The key is laying that solid foundation, building the basics.

SAP has truly found its own "sweet-spot" with this flywheel effect, and it's a concept that's resonating powerfully. SAP’s “fly-wheel” – Applications, Data and AI.

Apps, Data and AI

 

2. Application: Welcome (back) to the Business Suite. This time, suite-as-a-service.

"The application layer will be commoditized!" This isn't so far-fetched when you consider the landscape. The Cloud era has undeniably supercharged the "best-of-breed" SaaS world – if you have a business challenge, there’s almost certainly an app designed to tackle it. Infrastructure, while critical and pervasive, has also settled into a commodity status. And then there's AI, poised to take all of this into an entirely new orbit.

Imagine, anyone with a vision can now leverage AI and AI Agents to conjure up a SaaS application.

While Business Applications present a tougher nut to crack, they're no longer confined to the realm of impossibility for this new wave of creation.

SAP, foreseeing this trajectory, is making a significant bet: to bring a unifying cohesion to the myriads of heterogeneous "best-of-breed" solutions by championing a "best-of-suite" aka, ‘SAP Business Suite’ approach.

SAP Business Suite - as-a-Service

 

3. Data: A big-bang direction: SAP Business Data Cloud

Ultimately, every organization is on a quest to find that ideal path—to transform their transactional, critical systems into powerhouses of insight that fuel the very next decision. Yes, you could turn to workflow platform providers or lean on various Infrastructure platform providers. However, over time, a more enduring strategy makes sense: getting closer to the source of truth, ideally without the friction and complexity of constantly moving data.

This becomes especially compelling if such an approach comes with readily available, "out-of-the-box" insights, aptly named ‘Insight Apps.’

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)

 

4. AI: Joule, Joule Agents and AI Foundation

Last year, the plan was to streamline, at least, 80% of common daily tasks into Joule skills (i.e., Joule will help you do the tasks instead of you looking to find the exact Fiori page for said transaction). Currently, Joule has 1600 out-of-the-box skills!

Now, Joule Agents will be coming soon; they will connect these skills and introduce a new agentic workflow, where the fundamental way of executing daily tasks will be reimagined and simplified further, with agents able to do more and more tasks.

Both Joule and Joule Agents rely on a trusted AI foundation by SAP.

Also, the impact, as announced last year, is not limited to end-users. Joule is also available as SAP Joule for Consultants (GA Announced at Sapphire!) and SAP Joule for Developers.

Joule, Joule Agents, AI Foundation

 

5. Partnerships: Joule available everywhere.

The Microsoft Co-pilot <-> Joule integration was a highlight of last year. This year, in a real demo, we saw Joule working closely with other SaaS applications to truly make the customer experience seamless. We saw Joule in ServiceNow, where an urgent ticket highlighted a material defect. Joule, working along with WalkMe, summarized the issue and suggested finding a new supplier. Joule, in SAP Ariba, then took care of the supplier identification. For checking pricing, Joule talked with Gemini to search Gmail for better offers.

You get the idea. Joule is the co-pilot for enterprises, powered by SAP, and can work with the rest of the application stack that a customer might have.

Joule Everywhere

 

 

6. Perplexity – SAP Partnership: bringing unstructured data and answers to structured data

Perplexity is one of the killer apps of the GenAI Age. Perplexity defines itself as the ‘answer engine’. Instead of ten blue links, it gives you an answer that you can really trust, with citations.

The idea is – Perplexity provides this service to knowledge workers from the internet; SAP, and SAP’s Joule, do this for the Enterprise data realm. Together, customers get the best of both worlds in a managed approach. Lots to be seen as this partnership evolves.

 

7. Insight to Action TO Reason and Act

Insight to Action was a mantra – a key principle that brought us to the current state-of-affairs. Basically, you do advanced analytics, figure out insights, get wisdom from the data, and then do the next logical step, i.e., make actions.

The new world is even bolder: instead of you, the human, making judgments based only on your experience, your learning, your tenure, your role, your rank, etc., you now make those judgments supported by a vast system of AI models that help you “Reason” why things are happening (the insights). Armed with this, you are much more empowered to make sound Actions.

Reason + Act

 

 

8. Integrated Toolchain

The word ‘toolchain’ in simple English means – 1. Tools: basically, the items you need to get something fixed properly. Imagine them as the assets that a mechanic has at his disposal to fix your car. The wrench, the garage, the lift. And 2. Chain: it is not one tool that you need to fix your car but a couple of them working together.

The “Integrated Toolchain” from SAP is exactly that: several tools coming together to ‘fix’ your car. In this case, though, it's not about the car, but a move from your monolithic ERP system to a modular SAP Business Suite.

The tools in the Toolchain: Cloud ALM, Signavio, LeanIX, BTP, Build, and WalkMe.

Integrated Toolchain

 

9. Engagement Plans – Soft-launch

There is a simplified approach to SAP’s services and support model, which is critical to a lot of customers. The new model is a tiered model designed for simplicity and to supercharge the journey to Business AI.

This move streamlines and evolves familiar offerings like Enterprise Support, Preferred Success, and MaxAttention into three clear, progressive levels: Foundational, Advanced, and Max.

The Foundational plan, an evolution of Enterprise Support, gets everyone started, "turning on tooling" for AI and providing essential learning.

Advanced, inspired by Preferred Success: SAP will actively help "build and activate the embedded AI cases" in your cloud solutions.

Max – for a deep strategic partnership, the Max tier, echoing MaxAttention, helps you "define a holistic AI strategy and build prototypes for customer AI cases

Engagement Plans

entry.title: SuccessFactors Joule - Transaction, Navigational, RBP, Groups - How is this connected?
entry.content:
Welcome, SuccessFactors Admins!

If you’re reading this, you’re likely curious about how Joule can enhance your SuccessFactors experience and how SF Permissions and group assignments impact its use cases. This blog post is designed to provide you with a comprehensive understanding of Joule and its implications for both navigational and transactional use cases within SuccessFactors.

Through this post, we’ll delve into the fundamentals of Joule, explore the relationship between SF Permissions and Joule’s capabilities.

 

~~~~~~ Navigational Use Cases ~~~~~~ 
Navigational Use Cases helps your end users to navigate through the systems (incl. SuccessFactors). It can provide information to the end user but mostly they provide LINKS to applications.

But how does Joule knows for what SF applications you have permissions? Joule reacts on RBP and based on the RBP we know that deep links are allowed for the end user.

Have a look to the SF Deepling Guide to learn more about standard deep links.

Let us have a look to an example and let us pick a pure navigational case: "Show the org chart".

How it should look like:

Here we have a pure navigation - so based on my permissions Joule knows I'm allowed to open the org chart application and creates the link.

RBP's I need for this:

 

In the background - via the Refresh Synthetic Group Data Job, this user is assigned to a specific deep link permission group.

 

You can find this deep link groups here (Data Inspector -> Entry: Table_Users_Group)

 

In this list we are able find one group that indicates the access to the org chart "deepLinkActivationPermission_org_chart_view_org_chart"

 

As you can see on the screen shot this deep link role is assigned to 520 users.

The technical ID of this group is 7953 on this demo system.

 

During the Joule set up you have implemented an IPS Synch Job that synchs users and user assignments to WorkZone. In the settings for this IPS Job we have a filter in our source system to select only groups that are related to the deep link groups.

With this setting we makes sure, that we send only this group assignments to WorkZone (for Joule) that are relevant for deep link and navigation.

After running the Synch Job you can find the User-Group assignment in WorkZone. And here we should be able to find our group Id for the org chart deep link.

If I check my user, you can see a lot of assigned groups in WorkZone:

 

And here we see also our SuccessFactors Group for the org chart deep link:

 

Because we have the permission for this deep link, Joule shows us the link.

 

Let us have a look to an other example and let us pick a navigational case that also provides information: "Show my manager”.

This use case is a combination of:

- show information based on API Response

- generate the deep link to the user profile based on deep link permissions

 

What I often see is the following result:

As you can see, we see the name of the manager (API based) but not the button to navigate to the profile.

 

In this case the navigation them self is not established correctly.

So even the permission to access people profile is missing or the refresh synthetic group date job and/or the IPS job has not yet transferred the permission.

So to double check what is wrong, to the following checks:

  1. make sure the user has RBP based permission to people profile
  2. check what Group ID is generated for the people profile
  3. check if your user has this group assigned in WorkZone

 

In case the group is not assigned but RBPs are ok, re-run the SF Job and the IPS Job the get the user updated.

In case the group is assigned, and you can find it, the navigation should work.

 

Beside the navigation use cases that are based on the SF Deep Links (and the page access) we have some other navigation use cases where we do not have a dedicated SF deep link (e.g. show Employment Details, Show Compensation Details).

In such cases we have an dedicated check on the users permissions.

Here it is hard to give you an overview of all the relevant cases/permissions. But a good indication is the question "does the user has the option to run this action / open this page in the classical SuccessFactors UI? If yes, is there a corresponding Joule use cases?" If yes - the case should work. 

 

 

~~~~~~ Transactional Use Cases ~~~~~~ 

Transactional Use Cases allows your users to start transactions and process to generate, change, maintain data. Here we do not use this deep link groups. we stick to the APIs we are calling and to the results of the APIs.

We have the most transaction Use Cases in EC - please have a look to the blog post SuccessFactors Joule - Joule and EC Quick Actions - SAP Community to understand how EC, Quick Actions and EC RBPs are connected to Joule.

Here I want to highlight the Platform related transactional use cases to explain the relationship between Joule results and RBP. Please have a look to the overview of Platform Use Cases | SAP Help Portal.

Let us pick one "Show my department".

Show department is a pure platform use case (not an EC use case). So, we try to fetch the data from the Live Profile User / Employee Data NOT from JobInformation. Means, users need permission to see the department via Employee Data.

Please have in mind: In case you have EC the information from the JobProfile is synched via HRIS Synch to the Employee Data.

 

In case users have the permission, Joule will answer with my department details.

 

In case we do not have permissions, Joule will tell you, that Joule can't help because of missing configuration or permission. This is a typical answer in case the API response is empty.

 

One special hint on Joule scenarios using EC Quick actions. Quick actions respects your RBPs. So only people with access to a quick action can run the Joule scenario. Even the field level permissions are respected.

BUT if your user has the permission "Employee Central Foundation OData API (read-only)" this field lever permission is ignored. So plase make sure the end users has not to much permissions. 3458083 - Employee Central Quick Actions do not respect field-level permission

 

 

Other related blog posts:

SuccessFactors Joule - Joule and EC Quick Actions

entry.title: SAP User Experience in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
January 24 marked the general availability of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. As a cornerstone of modern enterprise, SAP Business Suite empowers organizations to scale, innovate, and adapt faster than ever. SAP cloud ERP applications, SAP Business AI, and SAP Business Data Cloud come together to deliver exceptional business value. But even the most robust cloud ERP wouldn't reach its full potential without a smooth user experience. That’s why we’re committed to evolving our product, listening to your feedback, and pushing the boundaries to deliver a user experience that matches the pace of today's dynamic work environment. Let me show you just how we achieve this in this release. Honorary mention: Generative AI is giving main character energy in this release.

Check out my video below to get a first glimpse into what’s coming in the area of SAP User Experience in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502:

 

Joule, the AI Copilot

Following a successful early adoption phase, Joule is generally available and fully integrated in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. This AI copilot utilizes generative AI to help users complete tasks more efficiently by using natural language to express their business needs. It offers quick navigation to applications, instant insights into key business data like purchase orders, and concise user enablement content to speed up tasks. Joule also simplifies personalization of the My Home section, allowing users to create cards with critical data for immediate insights. Its capabilities include recommending relevant applications, providing insightful information on business situations without needing to open the application, and offering brief summaries of help content. Users can directly create and add preview cards to the Insights section of My Home.

 

 

AI-Assisted Smart Summarization

AI-assisted smart summarization allows you to summarize object pages of SAP Fiori elements-based applications with the power of generative AI. The summarization can be reviewed and modified in a rich text editor and easily copied into productivity tools, such as email or chat, for follow-ups with co-workers and customers. The tool also facilitates efficient sharing through seamless copy-to-clipboard functionality for quick follow-ups. Additionally, users have the option to provide feedback on their experience with the feature, contributing to its continual improvement and adaptation to user needs.

 

 

AI-Assisted Easy Filter

The AI-assisted easy filter leverages state-of-the-art generative AI to redefine how users approach filtering within SAP Fiori elements-based list reports. Rather than setting filter values manually through type-ahead inputs and value helps, you can simply type your search query into the filter field. This approach not only enhances the user experience but also provides easy access to recent and favorite filter queries. The AI results are reliable, offering an intuitive and click-efficient experience, which is particularly beneficial for new users navigating the system.

 

 

Currently in Beta Testing: AI-Assisted Easy Enterprise Search

The AI-assisted easy enterprise search leverages generative AI to enable natural language querying for business objects throughout the solution. By simply typing your search query into the search field of the SAP Fiori launchpad, users can quickly access the information they need. This feature is especially valuable for its time-saving capabilities when searching with known criteria, providing reliable AI results and offering an intuitive, click-efficient experience that benefits new users in particular.

Register here to start testing: Newest AI-assisted user experience features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

Currently in Beta Testing: AI-Assisted Smart Personalization of My Home

The AI-assisted smart personalization of My Home allows you to use natural language through the power of generative AI to add insights cards to your My Home experience. This feature streamlines the process by enabling you to add insights cards directly from My Home with just two clicks, including a preview step for the end-user to review the AI-generated result. This tool offers several benefits, such as saving time when adding insights cards, providing better insights into business data, and delivering reliable AI results. It is designed to be intuitive and click-efficient, making it especially valuable for new users.

Register here to start testing: Newest AI-assisted user experience features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

Currently in Beta Testing: AI-Assisted Error Explanation

AI-assisted error explanation supports in understanding errors in SAP Fiori elements-based applications better and provides first recommendations on how to solve the errors by leveraging the power of generative AI. This innovative feature is seamlessly integrated into the current error visualization, enabling users to generate explanations on demand, which significantly saves time and improves workflow efficiency. By offering intuitive navigation, it delivers both the explanatory insight and recommended solutions within a single, click-efficient action. The process includes well-structured results that clarify the nature of the error and suggest practical resolutions.

Register here to start testing: Newest AI-assisted user experience features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

 

Interested in all things AI? Read this blog posts by my colleagues Yannick Peterschmitt and Sang Dao:

  • Artificial Intelligence and Other Intelligent ERP Use Cases in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
  • Artificial Intelligence & Technologies in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502

 

To-Dos in Microsoft Teams Integration

The integration of To-Dos in Microsoft Teams allows users to seamlessly access their workflow items from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition directly through the SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Microsoft Teams app. When you receive a workflow item in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, it triggers a notification in Microsoft Teams. This integration enables you to open the My Inbox application from Microsoft Teams to view details of the workflow items and to take immediate action. It also supports mobile approvals, ensuring flexibility in managing workflows on the go.

 

Smart Heads-Up of Situations in To Dos

Smart heads-up in To Dos ensure that you're always up to speed on situations requiring your attention, allowing you to manage all critical matters efficiently. This feature provides immediate insight and actionability through an intuitive interface, ensuring that nothing important slip through the cracks. By staying informed about and addressing potential issues early on, it significantly boosts productivity. Additionally, it offers support in prioritizing actions effectively. Users can get an overview of situations on both the standard and extended frameworks directly in the To-Dos section. With just one click, they can access detailed information by navigating to the My Situations and My Situations – Extended apps, depending on their needs.

 

As you can see, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 not only promises a transformed user experience but also enhances organizational productivity and strategic decision-making, bringing you closer to achieving seamless operational excellence, rooted in innovative technology.

Find out more about SAP User Experience in the numerous blog posts by my colleagues Thomas Reiss and Conrad Bernal.

The next release is just around the corner! See you in a bit. 😊

_____________________

The Future of Business, Today: Get Your Access to AI-Assisted Features Thanks to this Limited Promotion Now! 

The SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 update is a strategic investment that combines advanced AI with powerful enterprise management tools. Whether scaling your business, enhancing productivity, or building sustainable operations, its capabilities help support a more agile and resilient future.

Sign up for following features through the limited Promotion for AI-assisted Features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (for all countries, customers, and partners):

  • The easy filter and smart summarization features in SAP Fiori allow for efficient, natural language filtering of business data and seamless summarization capabilities, enabling users to quickly extract relevant information and jump-start subsequent collaboration. 
  • Facilitate easier understanding and interpretation of financial data with insights from cost center reviews and explanations of fixed-asset depreciation keys.

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Feel free to follow me on SAP Community and on LinkedIn to not miss any updates.

📽️ Watch the Replays of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!

From January 21 to January 24, we hosted a series of 21 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find a 43-minute-long recording and a presentation for SAP User Experience as well as 20 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime.

🔎Find All Our Assets

GROW with SAP is a comprehensive package designed to help businesses of all sizes to successfully adopt cloud ERP, anchored by SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for optimized processes and intelligent, sustainable operations. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: SAP Developer News May 22nd, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION 

Podcast: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/05/22/sap-developer-news-may-22nd-2025/ 

Live from the Sapphire Show Floor 

Enhanced SAP Build Capabilities (Part 1) 

  • SAP Joule Studio Blog post -  https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/build-deploy-and-extend-ai-agents-with-joule-studio/ba-p/14105964?source=social-Global-LinkedIn-No-BTP-Awareness-Announcement-Video&campaigncode=CRM-YA23-SMS-1941773&sprinklrid=17083384815  
  • SAP Sapphire Innovation guide – BTP - https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/innovation-guide/business-technology-platform.html 

 

SAP Architecture Center 

  • SAP Architecture Center online https://architecture.learning.sap.com/ 

 

Enhanced SAP Build Capabilities (Part 2) 

  • SAP Sapphire Innovation guide – BTP : https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/innovation-guide/business-technology-platform.html 
  • SAP Business Suite Pricing and Packaging : https://www.sap.com/products/business-suite/pricing.html 

 

Sapphire Special Edition 

  • SAP Sapphire Innovation Guide: https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/innovation-guide.html 
  • SAP Sapphire 2025: Notes from Day 1 Keynote with a focus on Developers: https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/sap-sapphire-2025-notes-from-day-1-keynote-with-a-focus-on-developers/bc-p/14107827#M49882 

AI Updates 

  • Business AI Innovation Guide: https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/innovation-guide/ai.html 
  • AI Foundation: https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/innovation-guide/ai.html#new-ai-foundation-provides-an-operating-system-for-sap-business-ai 
  • Prompt Optimizer: https://www.notdiamond.ai/blog/launching-prompt-adaptation 
  • Perplexity.ai: https://www.perplexity.ai/ 
  • WalkMe: https://www.sap.com/events/sapphire/innovation-guide/business-transformation-management.html#sap-introduces-new-walkme-digital-learning-solution-and-sap-business-suite-integration 

 

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 Live from the Sapphire Show Floor 

2:21 Enhanced SAP Build Capabilities (Part 1) 

5:51 SAP Architecture Center 

6:52 Enhanced SAP Build Capabilities (Part 2) 

8:28 AI Updates 

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for May 22nd, 2025. Special Sapphire Edition.

[Josh] Hey, everybody. Josh Bentley, Mr. Bluebeard, and representing the SAP Experience Center here on the Sapphire show floor. Now, what we've got is you come in and you learn about the different roles that you can do. You can be a CIO, CFO, different, different personas associated with all the technology SAP is talking about here at Sapphire. And once you do that, we create an AirTag that works with an Android or iOS phone. it gets custom built here and attached to your device so when you go home you can continue learning what you were actually exploring here on the show floor and if we go this way we've got the newest member of the sap community i want to show them to you he's AI powered by Joule i'm sure nice little robot there android and uh we got things going on with tons of business transformation AI uh supply chain over here we have an injection machine showing you how you can actually print the parts that were shown in the keynote and then this is how they come from the machine if it breaks you get the repair part actually from the warehouse so it's all automated and it's a great demo for all the customers here this is just one section of the Sapphire show floor i'm going to sneak out now the back and skip part of the experience center and go towards the learning and certification hub where everybody can actually go through and they can learn and take some tests on site and see what the certification programs are like on the way to that which is way back in the corner you can stop by any of the vendor booths that are happening and you've got plenty of vendors that we've known for years that are great trusted partners and customers and then we've got in the middle all the applications BTP all the sections that you can go to for let's say a different theater session I was talking to Sanya Leonard earlier and her team is doing great things with Abap cloud and so much is coming with that and AI and the innovations that we're doing in that space so again it's super busy here Sapphire This is the end of day three coming up, and what I want to get out of this is that you've got a ton of energy here in the community and a lot of things going on for not just developers, for everybody at SAP, but I'm heading back there to do some more work in the SAP Learning and Certification Hub. I'll talk to you later. Bye.

[Shrinivasan] Hello everybody it's an immense pleasure to bring you all the crucial highlights from SAP Sapphire 2025 annual conference that happened over last two days in Orlando Florida let's get this thing right off the bat Joule studio has been launched as part of SAP Build Solution Portfolio. Joule Studio has two key capabilities. Number one, Skill Builder Capability. Using this, you can design custom skills and execute predefined tasks within conversations triggered contextually by user intent. These skills produce specific results. They are configurable to the customer landscape. Skills can leverage other build components such as pre -built automations access to third -party integrations and AI models this feature will be made available by end of q2 this year number two agent builder capability using this you can create custom ai agents that can execute processes and workflows with varying adaptability and self-healing capabilities. Rather than following a set of prescribed instructions, agents leverage the thinking capability aspect of LLM to figure out how to solve a problem as well as APIs to augment their solution. Grounded in customer business data and SAP knowledge graph, these agents can deliver precise outputs and business-centric outcomes accommodating human inputs throughout the workflow. This feature will be made available Q4 this year. If you would like to know more details about Joule Studio, do read this blog post by SAP BTP expert Bharat Sandhu. Link is in the description box. That's not it. SAP announces new Joule capabilities for developers in SAP Build. These AI-assisted capabilities help empower developers at any skill level to build applications more quickly and easily. 1. SAP Build Work Zone Employees can work smarter with quick access provided by Joule to relevant apps, tasks, and processes from one central place. Users can further use Joule to query documents using document grounding capabilities. create new documents, and summarize them for relevant information needs. By providing AI responses based on SAP-relevant data from SAP Build Work Zone, employees can save time and stay productive. Number two, in SAP ABAP Cloud, whether it's migrating custom ABAP ECC code to SAP Cloud ERP to maintain a clean core or adapting existing legacy code to ABAP cloud model standards, these AI-driven enhancements can help developers be more productive and deliver precise outcomes faster. These two capabilities will be made available in Q3 this year. If you need more details, kindly refer to the BTP Innovation Guide provided in the description. Keep exploring and keep learning thank you.

[DJ] there's a new online resource for folks in the SAP tech ecosphere and that is the sap architecture center think of it as a one-stop shop for reference architectures on topics as diverse as event-driven application design multi-region resiliency federated machine learning and lots more why are reference architectures so important well think best practices think standardization scalability efficiency and interoperability and the best part about the new sap architecture center it's open open for collaboration for feedback and contributions using effectively the industry standard for collaboration on documentation and that is via GitHub. I've already made my first contribution. Dive in to the SAP Architecture Sensor now. See you online.

[Sheena M K] Beyond the exciting AI advancements around its supplier, SAP Build is bringing a host of expanded capabilities to help customers seamlessly extend their SAP business suite. Let's look at what they are. An enhanced Build Lobby which acts as the central hub for creating extension projects is now generally available which makes it easier than ever to get started. Extensibility Wizard, a guided tool which simplifies the process of building on stack and side -by-side extensions was made available in S4HANA public cloud edition at the end of last year. And here is more good news, by second half of 2025 this capability will also be available for SuccessFactors solutions. Data products from SAP Business Data Cloud will be accessible directly within SAP Build, supercharging application development while giving users richer data insights. This transformative integration is set to go live in second half of 2025. SAP Build is soon to support a wider range of event sources, enabling real-time dynamic interactions across multiple systems. Expect this feature to become generally available in the second half of 2025. SAP Build's streamlined pricing lets customers extend their business suite while giving developers the flexibility to choose the right tools without the hassle of managing multiple licenses. With these powerful new capabilities, SAP Build is set to transform how businesses create and scale extensions.

[Thomas] we've already heard from some of my colleagues about other ai enhancements that were announced at sapphire this week those affecting BTP the integration suite how Joule studio is being introduced inside of SAP build for extending Joule capabilities as well as how the business Data Cloud is the foundation of bringing business data into AI and making it more intelligent. But I'd like to talk to you now about some of the announcements that came that were part of the AI foundational layer. And right there, there was an announcement of a new version, new capabilities for AI Foundation itself. We see the current capabilities of AI Core and AI Launchpad being enhanced with new features like a new agent runtime. Or another example is the prompt optimizer to help you both optimize your existing prompts, but also help you write LLM independent prompts so that you're no longer tied with your prompts and your LLM generation overall to a particular LLM and that's through a partnership with Diamond AI. We'll be bringing the prompt optimizer to the BTP AI experience. We also saw another important partnership and that's with Perplexity. This is a tool that gives you an LLM-like experience but searching current information in the web. It's also really good at citing its sources and providing those in the output and this is an experience that we plan to bring to SAP Joule in the near future as well. So that was two super exciting partnerships that are going to enhance the AI capabilities of Joule and what you can build with AI on SAP BTP. And finally, we also saw the impact that the WalkMe acquisition is having to tie together Joule capabilities across your entire business, whether that's the SAP business suite or non-SAP capabilities as well. There's some really cool demos showing how ServiceNow was integrated with Ariba and S4. And Joule was able to tie that all together through the WalkMe capabilities. And like we said the other day in our little write-up after the day one keynote, This gives you a glimpse of some of the great things that are going to come to TechEd later this year because the announcements you hear at Sapphire are often the things we'll be able to show you exactly how you as developers are going to use later this year at TechEd. So be on the lookout for all that kind of information coming later this year.

entry.title: Understanding Joule - (Step #6) Document Grounding in Joule
entry.content:
This blog is part of the series Understanding Joule in 8 Baby Steps Please check the previous blogs, before proceeding.

 #1 – Introduction to Joule

#2 How Joule Works

#3 Joule Architecture

#4 Joule Interaction Patterns

#5 Joule in SAP SuccessFactors

In this blog, we will dive deep into Document Grounding in Joule.

 

Let's First Understand What Document Grounding is

Let’s understand this with an example.

If you ask Joule a question which relies on a company's internal information, for example:

“Can I use my private car for my business trip?”

You probably know the answer is in a policy documents, but don’t want to go through documents but just get answer using Joule.

This is exactly what Joule’s document grounding capability can do.

If Joule response this in usual way, by leveraging a LLM, then the response will be a generic one based on general SAP knowledge.

However....

If we feed Joule with company’s specific documents (like internal policies, reports and documents), then Joule can use “document grounding” and give more accurate and relevant answers.

 

So, what exactly is document grounding?

Document grounding is the process of making a LLM refer to specific documents to generate more accurate and relevant responses.

Instead of relying only on its pre-trained knowledge, the LLM pulls information from external sources to answer questions with real, up-to-date data.

 

Document Grounding in Joule

The document grounding capability of Joule helps us:

  • Upload documents in SAP and third-party repositories
  • And help Joule provide more comprehensive responses using information gathered from those documents

 

How does Document Grounding in Joule work?

Below image shows how document grounding works in Joule.

 

First customers need to setup the document grounding in Joule:

  1. Customers connect their document repositories via their own Microsoft SharePoint.
  2. Document Grounding AI Service is setup in SAP BTP.
  3. The Document Grounding AI Service takes the relevant customer-owned documents via Microsoft SharePoint and creates so called, vector embeddings.
  4. The vector embeddings are stored in SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine.

 

Once set up, this process runs once per day to ensure the latest document contents are always available to Joule.

When users can ask a query to Joule and flows work as below:

  1. User enter prompt in Joule UI and its send to Joule service on SAP BTP.
  2. Joule’s dialog manager sends the user question/prompt to its internal RAG service.
  3. The RAG service creates a vector “embedding” of the prompt. The vector embedding in simple words is - the vector of numbers that represents the question. The vector embedding is further used to perform a “vector query” against SAP HANA Vector Engine. The vector query is a kind of mathematical similarity search that returns sections of documents that are relevant to the users question. In this example, it might be sections from an employee manual detailing allowable trip expenses and reimbursement rates.
  4. SAP HANA Vector Engine returns the relevant document extract.
  5. The RAG service passes the relevant documents extracts back to the Joule Dialog Manager along with information about the source documents.
  6. The Dialog Manager appends the user question with the relevant extracts and sends the “enriched” prompt to the LLM.
  7. The LLM provide the response, “grounded” with the company-specific data and also appends links to the sources of the enrichment data.
  8. The Dialog Manager further sends the response along with links to sources, back to the Joule UI. The user gets a concise, reliable, natural language answer. They can optionally explore the links for quick verification and more details.

 

Current Scope of the document grounding capability in Joule

  • Data repository: Microsoft SharePoint
  • File format: PDF, Microsoft Word, HTML (Static),  JPEG,  JPG,  PNG,  TIFF,  TXT
  • Document content: Plain text (Tables and images are not supported)
  • Language: English
  • Content refresh: SharePoint content updated once every day

 

This is in nutshell - Document Grounding in Joule!

 

What's Next?

Blog #7 - Joule for Developers

Meanwhile, you may read the blog Joule, SAP’s Generative AI Copilot
